name: IKnowledgeProvider
description: Contract for knowledge architecture provider implementations. Provides structured access to knowledge bases with semantic querying, individual entry retrieval, and source catalog management. Supports RAG (Retrieval-Augmented Generation) patterns for enriching LLM context with domain knowledge.

methods.name: query

params.name: question
params.description: Natural language question to search the knowledge base

params.name: context
params.description: Optional context dict to refine the search (e.g., workflow metadata, domain hints)

methods.description: Query the knowledge base with a natural language question. Returns relevant knowledge entries sorted by confidence score (highest first). Optional context dict can refine results.

methods.name: get

params.name: key
params.description: Unique key identifying the knowledge entry

methods.description: Retrieve a specific knowledge entry by key. Returns null if the key does not exist in any knowledge source.

methods.name: list_sources
methods.description: List all registered knowledge sources. Returns metadata about each source including name, type, and URI. Useful for discovery and source management.

behavioral_expectations: Must implement structural subtyping (Protocol) — no explicit inheritance required
All methods are async — knowledge backends may involve I/O (database, API, file system)
KnowledgeEntry contains: content (str), source (str), confidence (float 0.0–1.0), metadata (dict)
query() results must be sorted by confidence descending (0.0–1.0 normalized)
query() with no matches must return an empty list (not raise)
get() must return null (not raise) for unknown keys
list_sources() must return all registered sources even if they contain no entries
Confidence scores must be normalized to 0.0–1.0 range across all sources
Context parameter in query() is optional — omitting it performs a context-free search
